title: centaurea sphaerocephala subsp. polyacantha
intro: Centaurea sphaerocephala subsp. polyacantha, also known as the globular knapweed or prickly knapweed, is a plant species within the thistle family, native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenes. The health benefits of Centaurea sphaerocephala subsp. polyacantha include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a potential natural remedy for minor injuries and infections. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
